goal: Eliminate manual workflow observation, reconciliation, health classification, transition explanation, transition-history maintenance, and trend interpretation while preserving fail-closed automation.
publication_chain: validate-chain-continuation generates and embeds the run receipt
full-validation-chain-report artifact transfers it to build-pages
build-pages publishes the embedded receipt into static/status
build-pages reconciles the current receipt with the prior public bounded history
build-pages classifies the reconciled history into a compact workflow health summary
build-pages emits a health-transition receipt from consecutive classified observations
build-pages reconciles the transition receipt into bounded deduplicated transition history
build-pages derives a bounded descriptive trend summary from transition history
verify-public-pages checks observation, health, transition, transition-history, and trend endpoints

history_policy.prior_history_unavailable_result: initialize a new bounded sequence without assigning a manual task

health_transition_policy.comparison: latest classified observation against immediately prior classified observation

health_transition_history_policy.prior_history_unavailable_result: initialize a new bounded sequence without assigning a manual task

cancelled_run_policy: A cancelled run is classified as transient or fail-closed and the next repository-owned trigger re-evaluates automatically.
external_evidence_policy: Missing source locators or specialist receipts are classified as externally deferred under their declared intake owners and do not become user tasks.
non_claims: This status proves automation contracts, not a successful future workflow run.
Public trend summaries are bounded and descriptive, not predictive.
It does not grant merge, release, deployment, publication, execution, or downstream mutation authority.
It does not convert missing external evidence into an acceptance result.
